title: abies religiosa subsp. colimensis
intro: Abies religiosa subsp. colimensis, also known as the Colim fir, is a subspecies of evergreen tree that belongs to the Abies religiosa species, native to Mexico. The Colim fir has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral compounds. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate headaches. Herbal preparations of Abies religiosa subsp. colimensis may be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal effects.
